findings.description: Escalation-contract-first sequencing: Phase 1 put Constraints/Escalation/Confidence in the fragments, mandated them in the fusion-prompt, and locked them with a source-level body-shape test — every later phase (2 recompiles, 20 new fusions) inherited the contract for free.

Context-lean codegen techniques kept huge waves reviewable: additive-injection recompile (preserve body + inject) and parallel-fusion offload (one subagent per new cell, writes to disk, returns one line) instead of from-scratch re-fusion in the orchestrator.
findings.evidence: Phase 1 U5 = 678 insertions / 0 deletions across 22 bodies; Phases 3-4 spawned 20 fusion subagents

findings.description: The axes.toml<->fusion-prompt drift guard (a test added in Phase 1 U3) forced lockstep updates in Phases 2b/3 exactly as designed — it caught the rename the moment it would have desynced the template and the schema.

Phase 2 was badly under-scoped by the PLAN: 'mechanical rename, grep-gated' was actually a 110-file cross-plugin atomic change with an internal PLAN contradiction (grep-gate 'zero residual' vs deliverable 'update skill references') that needed an operator decision mid-flight, plus collision hazards (planner contains plan; the loom .plan field).
findings.evidence: PR #188; AskUserQuestion on the whiteboard-skill-retirement scope

findings.description: L-004 turned the Live-spawn smokes into unpayable in-flight debt: agents authored in a session only register at the next session start, so the smokes deferred across all 6 hire/staff PRs and remain open at archive — the roster ships statically-verified but runtime-unverified.
findings.evidence: Live-spawn-smoke-deferred corrections in every hire/staff checkin

findings.description: A cluster of recurring papercuts cost re-work: my own loose test assertions (flagged by evaluator-test-unit nearly every early unit until internalized), the loom manifest-reconcile stranding (phase status committed after the unit commit), and the checkin schema rejecting a contract missing 'inputs' mid-commit.
findings.evidence: corrections in setup checkins 01-03; alignment + hire-4b corrections

findings.description: Fold axes.toml tool_grants into the codegen cache signature. The cache hashes only the fragments + fusion-prompt, so a grant edit shows as cache-hit and silently leaves the compiled tools: line stale; grant edits should invalidate the cache like fragment edits.
findings.evidence: Phase 5 had to hand-sync evaluator-css-architecture's tools after adding the grant

findings.description: A rename deliverable should map its full cross-plugin blast radius and resolve any grep-gate-vs-scope tension at PLAN time, not discover both mid-execution.

Sequence Live-spawn smokes as an explicit terminal step in the plan. Same-session smokes are structurally impossible (L-004), so a plan should account for a dedicated post-merge smoke session rather than letting each phase defer into a void.
findings.evidence: the accumulated smoke debt across this project

findings.description: Run the Live-spawn smokes for all 20 new agents (10 research-* + 10 implementer/fixer-*) in a fresh session: dispatch a sample across postures, confirm conformant output + the Constraints/Escalation/Confidence contract, record at learnings/session-notes/. This is the gate before the roster is runtime-trusted.
findings.evidence: AGENT-CODEGEN.md Live-spawn smoke checklist (refreshed in Phase 5)

findings.description: Two deferred substrate cleanups: the dangling .claude/agents/evaluator-base.md reference (deleted in a prior guild phase but still cited by agent bodies/packets; panel evaluators flagged it repeatedly), and the loom projects/<slug>/whiteboards/ artifact-directory convention (the one whiteboard-named surface left un-renamed in Phase 2, pending a loom-convention decision).
findings.evidence: Phase 1 U4 substrate observation; Phase 2 PR #188 'what's next'

findings.description: The downstream deployment plan depends on this one: it consumes the staffed roster and routes the operator-judgment-required outcome to a human (the loop-layer wiring this project deliberately left as a forward-compatible seam).
